under their noses. BY the control
Of Secretary Kraemer of the Sou
thern California Brewing associa-
ton, an officer in Los Angeles
brewery and an understudy Of Zee
handelaar, the local brewers we're
bluffed into letting Kraemer and
the Merchants and Manufacturer
association take charge of the ne
gotiations with the unions. Kraem-
er, even after a visit to San Fran
cisco, where the board of control
of the State Brewers advised him
to at least give the Los Angeles
unions a chance for a conference,
came back on the Job, announced
! that he was handling the fight
! without any outside assistance and
that there were no confabs with
"agitators" on the program.


STRIKE BREAKERS PROTECTED
After that, through the control
of the mayor, the council and the
police, strike breakers were pro-
tected at the expense Of the city,
an ordinance was passed that took
away the right Of the union men to
stand in front of a saloon that sold
non-union beer, to ask any strike
breaker to leave his job, or to lol-
ter, picket, carry or display ban
ners, signs or transparencies. You
even could not indirectly attempt
to influence any person to refrain
from buying anything. or even go.
ing anywhere, or from doing any
thing.


| It was about the tightest little,
Russian shutup-or-go-to-jail law
ever devised to make public opin-
ion null and void.


At once the union leaders called
on their men to volunteer to go to
jail. Just, as earlier in the year,
the I. w. w. leaders had asked
hundreds of their members to live
on bread and water in Spokane
bull pens In order that the Citizens
alliance law against free speech
might be tested.


In Los Angeles 50 men respond
ed and were jailed. Later they
were released on bail and recently
a jury set free the first Of these
men as guilty of no offense de-
spite the anti-picket ordinance.
